cost_model_ops.note: DEML(x,1)=exp(-x)=1n; add_pos requires provably positive operands

equations.name: Carnot efficiency

breakdown.note: 1-ratio in (0,1), provably positive; sub used because operands are constants/bounded

cross_domain_isomorphisms: Greenshields traffic flow: 1 - k/k_jam = 4n
Fick diffusion normalized: 1 - C/C_0 = 4n
All '1 - ratio' two-input structures share 4n
equations.key_observation: Simplest two-input thermodynamic efficiency. Universal '1-ratio' pattern at 4n.

equations.name: Boltzmann entropy
cross_domain_isomorphisms: Shannon entropy per term: p*ln(p) — same 3n structure (constant * ln)
Information-theoretic entropy: same formula, different constant
Thermodynamic entropy = Shannon entropy * (k_B / nats-to-bits scaling)
equations.key_observation: Cheapest fundamental physics equation alongside F=ma. 3n is the floor for constant*transform(x).

equations.name: Clausius-Clapeyron equation (ideal-gas approximation)
cross_domain_isomorphisms: van't Hoff (see thermo_04): same T^2 denominator pattern
Reaction rate vs temperature: exponential T-P coupling
equations.key_observation: 9n driven by T^2 (3n pow). The pow-mul-mul-div motif is the 9n quadratic-denominator pattern.

equations.name: van't Hoff equation

breakdown_ln_ratio.note: sign depends on T ordering; sub is general

cross_domain_isomorphisms: Clausius-Clapeyron: both involve temperature-pressure/equilibrium relationships
Arrhenius: both use exp(-deltaE/RT) structure, same Boltzmann factor
equations.key_observation: 10n for the log-ratio form. 13n for full K2. Two recip + sub for temperature difference is the core 4n sub-pattern.

equations.name: Ideal gas law
cross_domain_isomorphisms: Ohm's law extended: V=IR → P=nRT/V same mul-mul-div motif
Power law: P*V = nRT is the bilinear product form

equations.name: Gibbs free energy

breakdown.note: TdeltaS can be positive or negative
deltaG can be positive or negative; sub=2n handles signed case

cross_domain_isomorphisms: Carnot efficiency: both 4n, both use sub as final op
Helmholtz free energy A = U - TS: identical structure at 4n
Net profit = revenue - cost: same 4n economic structure
equations.key_observation: 4n: mul-sub pattern. Same as Carnot despite different physical meaning. Free energy equations are universally 4n.

equations.name: Arrhenius equation
cross_domain_isomorphisms: Beer-Lambert: I = I0*exp(-alpha*x) — 5n (no division, alpha*x direct)
Arrhenius is Beer-Lambert + division: 5n + 2n div = 7n
Population decay: N = N0*exp(-lambda*t) — 5n family
Compound interest: A = P*exp(rt) — 5n (no neg, no div)
All exponential decay with computed exponent: 7n family
equations.key_observation: 7n: the div-DEML variant of the 5n exponential family. DEML(x,1) saves 1n vs exp+neg.

equations.name: Stefan-Boltzmann law
cross_domain_isomorphisms: Clausius-Clapeyron: both 9n, both powered by a single pow(T) term
Any constant * T^4: pow(3n) + 3x mul(2n each) = 9n
Planck radiation peak (Wien's law variant): similar T^4/T^5 structure
equations.key_observation: 9n: pow dominates. Three constants collapsed into mul-mul-mul chain. T^4 = T^2*T^2 could be 2+2=4n via repeated squaring, but pow=3n is canonical.

equations.name: Maxwell-Boltzmann energy distribution
equations.total_cost_note: Partial-constant prefactor: only T-dependent parts counted after folding pi and 2*pi into constants

steps.note: pi*k_B treated as precomputed constant; only T^1.5 varies
kT requires mul(k_B,T)=2n if T varies; folded into prefactor group

breakdown.note: div at step 4 uses kT from prefactor computation

equations.key_observation: 13n: two multiplicative structures (T-prefactor + E-factor) joined by mul. pow(T,1.5) and sqrt(E) both required simultaneously.

equations.name: Entropy of mixing (N components)

combining.correction_note: If neg is applied once to sum rather than per-term: neg(sum)=2n, then 4N + 3(N-1) + 2 + 2 = 7N - 1

equations.preferred_strategy: Compute sum of x_i*ln(x_i) (4n per term: ln+mul), then neg once, then mul by nR

preferred_breakdown_per_term.note: sign is negative but mul handles signed
terms are negative; add_gen required for inter-term accumulation

equations.preferred_note: Using add_gen(11n) per accumulation step if signs mixed, or restructure with neg-per-term to use add_pos(3n). The 7N-1 figure uses neg-once strategy with add_pos accumulation.
cross_domain_isomorphisms: Shannon entropy: H = -sum p_i*log(p_i) — identical structure, 7N-3 (no leading constant mul)
Shannon * constant scaling: entropy_of_mixing = n*R * Shannon_entropy
Kullback-Leibler divergence: similar sum-of-log-ratios motif
equations.key_observation: 7N-1: linear in N. Shannon entropy is 7N-3 (same per-term cost, no final mul). Entropy of mixing IS Shannon entropy * (nR).

cross_domain_isomorphism_catalog.note: Floor cost for any constant-scaled logarithmic quantity
Exponent is a direct product; no division needed

cross_domain_isomorphism_catalog.pattern: pow + mul-chain or pow + mul + mul + div
cross_domain_isomorphism_catalog.note: pow(3n) dominates; both are 9n despite different structures
Identical structure regardless of thermodynamic potential

cross_domain_isomorphism_catalog.pattern: per term: ln + mul; N terms + (N-1) add_pos + [optional mul]
cross_domain_isomorphism_catalog.note: Entropy of mixing = Shannon * nR; differs by one mul at end

cost_summary_table.pattern: recip + recip + sub + mul + neg + mul
pow + recip + sqrt + div + DEML + mul + mul
